correctness.notes: Theorem 1 sharpness/minimax step for \label{thm:nested-endpoint}: The reproduced admissible weights must come from one joint treatment rule p_a(U) with sum_a p_a(U)=1. Equivalently, for each x, r_a(y)=e_a(x)/E[p_a(U)|X=x,Y(a)=y] and the actionwise weights must be jointly compatible. The note replaces this by the product class {ell_a<=r_a<=u_a, E[r_a|A=a,X=x]=1} and then separates the supremum actionwise; it drops the compatibility constraint sum_a e_a(x)/r_a(Y(a))=1 at the latent-unit level.; Theorem 3 fixed-eta nuisance expansion for \label{thm:onestep-limit}: The note keeps only tau^{-1}(P_n-P){phi_{eta_*,pi}(O;nu_*)}. Reproduction gives the additional first-order drift tau^{-1} sqrt(n) P{phi_{eta_*,pi}(hat nu)-phi_{eta_*,pi}(nu_*)}. At fixed eta this includes E_X sum_a pi_a(X)[hat mu_a(X)-m_a(X)] plus only a product term {1-e_a/hat e_a}{hat m_a-hat mu_a}; hat mu_a-m_a is first order unless the MSM transform is itself orthogonalized or estimated at o_p(n^{-1/2}).; Theorem 3 propensity contribution to the MSM tilted-loss operator: The score orthogonalizes the inverse-propensity denominator but omits the first-order derivative of the endpoint with respect to e_a through ell_a=e_a+(1-e_a)/Gamma and u_a=e_a+Gamma(1-e_a). The missing influence term is tau^{-1} sum_a pi_a(X) dot T_{eta_*,a,e}(X){1{A=a}-e_a(X)}, where dot T_{eta,a,e}=partial_{e_a} mathfrak T_Gamma^+{(Y-eta)_+|a,X}; in a binary generated-loss cell this derivative is p(1-Gamma) or -(1-p)(1-1/Gamma) on the two active branches.; Theorem 3 definition of zeta_{eta,a} and pathwise derivative of mathfrak T_Gamma^+: The note defines zeta_{eta,a} only by the mean identity E[zeta_{eta,a}|A=a,X=x]=mathfrak T_Gamma^+{g_eta|a,x}. A mean-normalized tilted loss is not enough to identify the pathwise derivative. For the bounded-weight LP the derivative uses the dual-threshold pseudo-outcome, schematically lambda_{eta,a}+r^*_{eta,a}{g_eta(Y)-lambda_{eta,a}}, not an arbitrary transform with the correct conditional mean.; Theorem 3 eta-selection/envelope cancellation: Reproduction gives the term sqrt(n)(hat eta-eta_*){1+tau^{-1} partial_eta Psi^+_{eta_*}} plus the empirical-process increment tau^{-1}sqrt(n)(P_n-P){phi_{hat eta,pi}-phi_{eta_*,pi}}. The note asserts these are o_p(1), but Assumption unique-eta only says the optimizer is unique and left/right derivatives straddle zero; it allows kink cases and does not assume differentiability, curvature, consistency/rate of hat eta, or uniform stochastic equicontinuity.

perItemFindings.label: nested-endpoint
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 1 sharpness/minimax step for \label{thm:nested-endpoint}: The reproduced admissible weights must come from one joint treatment rule p_a(U) with sum_a p_a(U)=1. Equivalently, for each x, r_a(y)=e_a(x)/E[p_a(U)|X=x,Y(a)=y] and the actionwise weights must be jointly compatible. The note replaces this by the product class {ell_a<=r_a<=u_a, E[r_a|A=a,X=x]=1} and then separates the supremum actionwise; it drops the compatibility constraint sum_a e_a(x)/r_a(Y(a))=1 at the latent-unit level. → The displayed nested functional is reproduced as a valid upper bound, but not as the sharp upper endpoint. It can overstate sup_Q CVaR_tau{Y_Q(pi)} for multi-action policies unless an additional joint-realizability lemma or stronger model restriction is supplied.

perItemFindings.label: onestep-limit
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 3 fixed-eta nuisance expansion for \label{thm:onestep-limit}: The note keeps only tau^{-1}(P_n-P){phi_{eta_*,pi}(O;nu_*)}. Reproduction gives the additional first-order drift tau^{-1} sqrt(n) P{phi_{eta_*,pi}(hat nu)-phi_{eta_*,pi}(nu_*)}. At fixed eta this includes E_X sum_a pi_a(X)[hat mu_a(X)-m_a(X)] plus only a product term {1-e_a/hat e_a}{hat m_a-hat mu_a}; hat mu_a-m_a is first order unless the MSM transform is itself orthogonalized or estimated at o_p(n^{-1/2}). → The claimed product-rate nuisance condition is insufficient for asymptotic linearity with the stated influence function; the estimator can have first-order bias from estimating the least-favorable transform.

perItemFindings.label: onestep-limit
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 3 propensity contribution to the MSM tilted-loss operator: The score orthogonalizes the inverse-propensity denominator but omits the first-order derivative of the endpoint with respect to e_a through ell_a=e_a+(1-e_a)/Gamma and u_a=e_a+Gamma(1-e_a). The missing influence term is tau^{-1} sum_a pi_a(X) dot T_{eta_*,a,e}(X){1{A=a}-e_a(X)}, where dot T_{eta,a,e}=partial_{e_a} mathfrak T_Gamma^+{(Y-eta)_+|a,X}; in a binary generated-loss cell this derivative is p(1-Gamma) or -(1-p)(1-1/Gamma) on the two active branches. → The displayed influence function is incomplete whenever Gamma>1 and the MSM bound depends nontrivially on the nominal propensity, so the variance formula targets the wrong limit law.

perItemFindings.label: onestep-limit
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 3 definition of zeta_{eta,a} and pathwise derivative of mathfrak T_Gamma^+: The note defines zeta_{eta,a} only by the mean identity E[zeta_{eta,a}|A=a,X=x]=mathfrak T_Gamma^+{g_eta|a,x}. A mean-normalized tilted loss is not enough to identify the pathwise derivative. For the bounded-weight LP the derivative uses the dual-threshold pseudo-outcome, schematically lambda_{eta,a}+r^*_{eta,a}{g_eta(Y)-lambda_{eta,a}}, not an arbitrary transform with the correct conditional mean. → The score is under-specified and may not be an influence function for the sharp MSM operator; the one-step theorem needs the actual canonical pseudo-outcome or an explicit differentiability lemma.

perItemFindings.label: onestep-limit
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 3 eta-selection/envelope cancellation: Reproduction gives the term sqrt(n)(hat eta-eta_*){1+tau^{-1} partial_eta Psi^+_{eta_*}} plus the empirical-process increment tau^{-1}sqrt(n)(P_n-P){phi_{hat eta,pi}-phi_{eta_*,pi}}. The note asserts these are o_p(1), but Assumption unique-eta only says the optimizer is unique and left/right derivatives straddle zero; it allows kink cases and does not assume differentiability, curvature, consistency/rate of hat eta, or uniform stochastic equicontinuity. → The minimization step can contribute at first order or yield a nonstandard limit under the stated assumptions; the asymptotic linearity claim needs stronger local smoothness and argmin conditions.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[blocking, conjecture:nested-endpoint] Theorem 1 sharpness/minimax step for \label{thm:nested-endpoint}: The reproduced admissible weights must come from one joint treatment rule p_a(U) with sum_a p_a(U)=1. Equivalently, for each x, r_a(y)=e_a(x)/E[p_a(U)|X=x,Y(a)=y] and the actionwise weights must be jointly compatible. The note replaces this by the product class {ell_a<=r_a<=u_a, E[r_a|A=a,X=x]=1} and then separates the supremum actionwise; it drops the compatibility constraint sum_a e_a(x)/r_a(Y(a))=1 at the latent-unit level.. Fix: The displayed nested functional is reproduced as a valid upper bound, but not as the sharp upper endpoint. It can overstate sup_Q CVaR_tau{Y_Q(pi)} for multi-action policies unless an additional joint-realizability lemma or stronger model restriction is supplied.
[serious, conjecture:onestep-limit] Theorem 3 fixed-eta nuisance expansion for \label{thm:onestep-limit}: The note keeps only tau^{-1}(P_n-P){phi_{eta_*,pi}(O;nu_*)}. Reproduction gives the additional first-order drift tau^{-1} sqrt(n) P{phi_{eta_*,pi}(hat nu)-phi_{eta_*,pi}(nu_*)}. At fixed eta this includes E_X sum_a pi_a(X)[hat mu_a(X)-m_a(X)] plus only a product term {1-e_a/hat e_a}{hat m_a-hat mu_a}; hat mu_a-m_a is first order unless the MSM transform is itself orthogonalized or estimated at o_p(n^{-1/2}).. Fix: The claimed product-rate nuisance condition is insufficient for asymptotic linearity with the stated influence function; the estimator can have first-order bias from estimating the least-favorable transform.
[serious, conjecture:onestep-limit] Theorem 3 propensity contribution to the MSM tilted-loss operator: The score orthogonalizes the inverse-propensity denominator but omits the first-order derivative of the endpoint with respect to e_a through ell_a=e_a+(1-e_a)/Gamma and u_a=e_a+Gamma(1-e_a). The missing influence term is tau^{-1} sum_a pi_a(X) dot T_{eta_*,a,e}(X){1{A=a}-e_a(X)}, where dot T_{eta,a,e}=partial_{e_a} mathfrak T_Gamma^+{(Y-eta)_+|a,X}; in a binary generated-loss cell this derivative is p(1-Gamma) or -(1-p)(1-1/Gamma) on the two active branches.. Fix: The displayed influence function is incomplete whenever Gamma>1 and the MSM bound depends nontrivially on the nominal propensity, so the variance formula targets the wrong limit law.
[serious, conjecture:onestep-limit] Theorem 3 definition of zeta_{eta,a} and pathwise derivative of mathfrak T_Gamma^+: The note defines zeta_{eta,a} only by the mean identity E[zeta_{eta,a}|A=a,X=x]=mathfrak T_Gamma^+{g_eta|a,x}. A mean-normalized tilted loss is not enough to identify the pathwise derivative. For the bounded-weight LP the derivative uses the dual-threshold pseudo-outcome, schematically lambda_{eta,a}+r^*_{eta,a}{g_eta(Y)-lambda_{eta,a}}, not an arbitrary transform with the correct conditional mean.. Fix: The score is under-specified and may not be an influence function for the sharp MSM operator; the one-step theorem needs the actual canonical pseudo-outcome or an explicit differentiability lemma.
[serious, conjecture:onestep-limit] Theorem 3 eta-selection/envelope cancellation: Reproduction gives the term sqrt(n)(hat eta-eta_*){1+tau^{-1} partial_eta Psi^+_{eta_*}} plus the empirical-process increment tau^{-1}sqrt(n)(P_n-P){phi_{hat eta,pi}-phi_{eta_*,pi}}. The note asserts these are o_p(1), but Assumption unique-eta only says the optimizer is unique and left/right derivatives straddle zero; it allows kink cases and does not assume differentiability, curvature, consistency/rate of hat eta, or uniform stochastic equicontinuity.. Fix: The minimization step can contribute at first order or yield a nonstandard limit under the stated assumptions; the asymptotic linearity claim needs stronger local smoothness and argmin conditions.

Referee notes: I reproduced the three load-bearing routes: the nested MSM-CVaR sharpness/minimax step, the pointwise-band finite LP comparison, and the one-step expansion. The sharp endpoint and one-step limit law do not reproduce as stated; the band finite-class calculation reproduces modulo its dependence on the failed endpoint/shared regularity.
